name: Lumin University
gate_blockers: Legal review required — using 'university' without accreditation has state-specific legal implications
Competency standards per domain not yet defined
Credential verification technology not selected (blockchain vs. cryptographic hash vs. third-party)
Conflict of interest management (platform as educator AND evaluator) needs structural solution
assertions: Students who know how they learn outperform students who don't, regardless of raw ability
Competency-based credentials are more meaningful to employers than time-based degrees
The most durable learning happens in the context of real stakes, not simulated ones
Kids OS graduates will enter higher education with measurably better self-knowledge than traditional students
anti_drift_notes: Competency-based means NO seat time credit — you demonstrate or you don't have the credit
Hardship Protocol applies here exactly as in Kids OS — access is never suspended
Life as the curriculum is not a metaphor — actual life decisions ARE the coursework
Pre-accreditation credentials must be transparently labeled as such
Platform cannot be both educator and sole evaluator without conflict-of-interest management
Peer network is curated for complementarity, not affinity — diversity of perspective is a feature
decision_debt: Legal name — 'Lumin University' may require accreditation in some states before use
Accrediting body selection — regional vs. national vs. specialized accreditor
Credential verification technology — tamper-proof issuance system
Evaluator independence — how to manage platform's dual role as educator and evaluator
Transfer credit policy — how does work done elsewhere map to Lumin competencies?
Employer partnership model — how do employers recognize Lumin credentials before accreditation?
candidate_concepts: Corporate training track (employer-funded, workforce competency credentialing)
International cohorts (students from different countries, cross-cultural learning design)
Alumni network (post-graduation community with ongoing platform access)
Research arm (outcome tracking that generates publishable evidence for accreditation case)
Graduate-level tracks (master's-equivalent competency programs in specific domains)
